_meta.description: Published likelihood-of-approval (LOA) benchmarks transcribed from the BIO/QLS 2021 report, used by tests/test_benchmarks.py to validate that the engine's compounded PoS reproduces the sources' own published figures. This is BENCHMARK GROUND TRUTH, not engine input: it is never read by the engine and is not part of the baseline_rates.json build.
_meta.source: BIO, QLS Advisors, Informa Pharma Intelligence (2021). Clinical Development Success Rates and Contributing Factors 2011-2020.
_meta.transcription_note: Values read directly from the report's figure tables (pypdf used once as a transcription aid; not an engine dependency). LOA is BIO's own compounded probability of FDA approval FROM the given phase. NDA/BLA cells reported as 100% appear here verbatim; the engine clips probabilities to [0.1%, 99%], so it reports 0.99 where a source cell is 1.000 (a deliberate Day-2 decision -- see docs/validation.md).
_meta.phase_meaning: LOA from Phase I / Phase II / Phase III / NDA-BLA to approval

fig5b_loa._ref: BIO/QLS 2021 Figure 5b (likelihood of approval by disease area)

fig10b_modality_loa._ref: BIO/QLS 2021 Figure 10b (LOA from Phase I by drug modality)
